title: Jeera Rice

subs.name: any long-grain white rice, adjust water by 2 tbsp

steps.text: Wash the basmati rice three times until the water runs clear, then soak in cold water for 20 minutes. This step shortens the cooking time and helps the grains stay separate. Drain before cooking.

Heat ghee in a heavy-bottomed saucepan over medium flame. Once the ghee shimmers, add cumin seeds and the bay leaf. Let them sizzle for 30 seconds until the cumin turns a shade darker and becomes aromatic.
Add the drained rice and stir gently for 1 minute to coat every grain with ghee. This toasting step adds flavour and helps the cooked grains separate.
Pour in 2.5 cups of water and add salt. Bring to a full boil over high heat, then immediately reduce to the lowest flame, cover tightly and cook for 12 minutes. Do not lift the lid during this time.
Turn off the heat and let the rice steam undisturbed with the lid on for 5 minutes. This carryover steaming finishes the cooking gently and ensures fluffy, separate grains.
Fluff gently with a fork, discarding the bay leaf. Garnish with fresh coriander leaves and serve with any dal, rajma, or paneer gravy.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.

substitution_notes: ghee → butter or neutral oil; basmati-rice → any long-grain white rice, adjust water by 2 tbsp
